source_record_title: Two Investment Firms Charged with Making False Claims of Artificial Intelligence Capabilities in Case of AI Washing
local_case_titles: SEC settlement with Delphia over false and misleading statements about AI and machine learning use ; SEC settlement with Global Predictions over false and misleading AI financial-adviser claims
collision_reason: AIID incident 653 collapses Delphia and Global Predictions into one combined SEC AI-washing record with one shared report and one shared penalty total, while LIMEN currently keeps two separate settled-order rows with company-specific wording and penalties.
reviewer_safe_delta: Leaves a high-value negative result: there is visible cross-database same-event-family coverage beyond the current singleton AIID pilot, but it is aggregation-shaped rather than row-exact under current LIMEN join discipline.
still_not_safe: Do not count AIID incident 653 as two exact overlaps or as a canonical second pilot until LIMEN explicitly supports one-to-many comparator linkage rules and shared-surface denominator handling for split local rows.
paper_thesis_use: Methods/results limitation note for Table 6A, Result block 7A, and thesis discussion of comparator exactness versus aggregation.
visualization_dashboard_hook: Comparator collision inset: external aggregate record vs split local regulator-order rows.
next_smallest_publishability_move: If shard-002 work resumes, either formalize a reviewer-safe one-to-many overlap rule with denominator controls or keep searching for a second true one-row-to-one-row AIID/OECD overlap.

source_record_title: SEC Fines Investment Firms for Misleading AI Claims in 'AI Washing' Crackdown
local_case_titles: SEC settlement with Delphia over false and misleading statements about AI and machine learning use ; SEC settlement with Global Predictions over false and misleading AI financial-adviser claims
collision_reason: OECD incident 2024-03-18-0cfc also aggregates Delphia and Global Predictions into one record keyed to the joint SEC action and shared USD 400000 framing, while LIMEN preserves separate company-level settled-order rows.
reviewer_safe_delta: Shows that official metadata-first comparator coverage can surface the same aggregation problem as AIID, so a tempting second OECD overlap should still remain non-promoted under current exact-row rules.
still_not_safe: Do not reinterpret this single OECD incident as two populated LIMEN join slots, unrestricted official interoperability, or a reason to relax row-level exactness thresholds.
paper_thesis_use: Official-comparator methods note, reviewer appendix on exactness, and thesis paragraph on metadata-first aggregation collisions.
visualization_dashboard_hook: AIID/OECD asymmetry panel with shared one-to-many collision state.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Either formalize one-to-many comparator handling with explicit split-row controls or keep OECD at one exact pilot plus one visible-but-nonpromoted aggregate collision.

source_record_title: SEC AI-washing split-row comparator collision audit
local_case_titles: SEC settlement with Delphia over false and misleading statements about AI and machine learning use ; SEC settlement with Global Predictions over false and misleading AI financial-adviser claims
collision_reason: Both AIID and OECD expose one visible external record for the joint SEC AI-washing action, but neither can be promoted safely under the current LIMEN rule that one exact overlap pilot should map one external record to one local row.
reviewer_safe_delta: Adds publication-useful negative evidence that the next comparator gain is not automatically every visible same-event-family record; aggregation shape matters.
still_not_safe: This cycle does not change any join count, evidence tier, or shared claim. It only narrows future route selection and blocks over-eager occupancy inflation.
paper_thesis_use: Route-selection memo for comparator exactness, manuscript limitations, and thesis interoperability governance.
visualization_dashboard_hook: Threshold board sidecar distinguishing row-exact pilots from one-to-many aggregate collisions.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prefer either a true one-row-to-one-row overlap elsewhere or an explicit methods note/ledger governing aggregate external records before any shared promotion.
